Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Restarted testing due to LU-7056 failure.

Is there any test for the ldev utility?  This isn't something we test ourselves, and it only has the initial commit from Ned.  I'm not against landing the patch when it is ready but we have no way to validate this change, so it would be a lot better to have a test case of some sort, probably in conf-sanity.sh.

Also, there should be a corresponding update to lustre/doc/ldev.8 for this new option.

Patch Set 1:

> Restarted testing due to LU-7056 failure.
 > 
 > Is there any test for the ldev utility?  This isn't something we
 > test ourselves, and it only has the initial commit from Ned.  I'm
 > not against landing the patch when it is ready but we have no way
 > to validate this change, so it would be a lot better to have a test
 > case of some sort, probably in conf-sanity.sh.
 > 
 > Also, there should be a corresponding update to lustre/doc/ldev.8
 > for this new option.

 > Restarted testing due to LU-7056 failure.
 > 
 > Is there any test for the ldev utility?  This isn't something we
 > test ourselves, and it only has the initial commit from Ned.  I'm
 > not against landing the patch when it is ready but we have no way
 > to validate this change, so it would be a lot better to have a test
 > case of some sort, probably in conf-sanity.sh.
 > 
 > Also, there should be a corresponding update to lustre/doc/ldev.8
 > for this new option.

There are two options for writing a test case. I could provide a sample ldev.conf and nids file in the test directory or I could leverage the test environment's MGSNID. Which would you feel is the better option?

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.6/x86_64: (https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sessions/cef187a0-50c1-11e5-92be-5254006e85c2).  Ran 8 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 1:

Giuseppe, for testing in our autotest environment I think it would make sense to generate a ldev.conf file based on the environment variables set in lustre/cfg/local.sh (so that the hostnames, devices, etc exist) and then use ldev to configure a test filesystem (conf-sanity.sh tests can reformat the filesystem if needed).

Patch Set 1:

> Giuseppe, for testing in our autotest environment I think it would
 > make sense to generate a ldev.conf file based on the environment
 > variables set in lustre/cfg/local.sh (so that the hostnames,
 > devices, etc exist) and then use ldev to configure a test
 > filesystem (conf-sanity.sh tests can reformat the filesystem if
 > needed).

Andreas, wouldn't it be sufficient to generate an ldev.conf based on the environment variables and run a simple ldev "echo %m" and then ensure the correct value is echoed?

Patch Set 1:

That would be enough to verify that the newly added functionality of this patch works. However, there aren't any other tests of the ldev code, so there isn't any way to know if the new patch has broken it. That is very unlikely looking at this patch, so if be OK to land the patch with the minimal test you describe. 

I'm not suggesting that full ldev testing is a requirement for landing this patch, but it is something for you to consider for a separate patch to start testing the rest of the ldev functionality. Possibly in the long term the existing "llmount.sh" and lustre/tests/cfg could be moved over to ldev, but it makes sense to start with some basic tests of its functionality.

commit.subject: LU-7060 ldev: Added MGS NID substitution to ldev
commit.message:
LU-7060 ldev: Added MGS NID substitution to ldev

For automation purposes, it would be nice to have a
command substitution for the MGS NID of a node.
The ldev perl script now has a %m flag to sub in
the MGS NID. The MGS associated with a node is
determined by which file system the node is a part
of. The NID is obtained by using the current
NID file parsing. Also, MGS is now an acceptable
type to have specified in a label.

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Di Natale <dinatale2@llnl.gov>
Change-Id: Ifcfad7664ac028e2074030e4b3ba5995ddb5358d
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/16129
